_comment: AUDIT TEST CASE: Multiple ENCL entries only (no REF) - tests ENCL numbering format, alignment, and spacing
subj: AUDIT TEST FOR MULTIPLE ENCLOSURE ENTRIES WITH LONG TEXT TO FORCE LINE WRAPPING AND VERIFY ALIGNMENT
encl: (1) Records Management Procedures Manual with comprehensive guidance on filing and retrieval operations
(2) Correspondence Preparation Handbook covering formatting standards for all types of military letters
(3) Implementation Timeline Chart showing key milestones for the new records management system deployment
(4) Training Schedule and Attendance Roster for upcoming records management certification courses
body: 1. This is the first paragraph of the audit test case for multiple ENCL entries. The purpose of this document is to verify that the renderer correctly handles multiple enclosure entries with long text that forces line wrapping.
2. This is the second paragraph. Each ENCL entry above contains intentionally long text to test whether continuation lines return to the left margin rather than being indented under the marker text.
3. This is the third paragraph. The audit will verify that ENCL entries use the correct numbering format with Arabic numerals in parentheses: (1), (2), (3), (4).

4. This is the fourth paragraph. The spacing between the subject line and the first ENCL entry should be exactly one leading unit (14.4 points), and the spacing between consecutive ENCL entries should also be one leading unit.

5. This is the fifth and final paragraph. Since there are no REF entries in this test case, the spacing from the subject line to the first ENCL entry should be one leading unit, and the spacing from the last ENCL entry to the first body paragraph should be exactly one leading unit (14.4 points).
